get.description: Liveness / readiness probe.

put.description:
Update an existing series. Only provided (non-None) fields change.

``content_format`` and ``aspect_ratio`` are immutable once any
episode has moved past ``draft`` because they're read by both the
script and assembly steps from different rows — changing them
silently mis-renders existing episodes on reassemble. The caller
must clone the series instead.

post.description:

post.description: Stores ``music_enabled``, ``music_mood``, and ``music_volume_db`` in the episode's ``metadata_`` JSONB under the ``music_settings`` key. When ``reassemble`` is ``true`` (the default), a reassembly job is also enqueued so the change takes effect immediately in the output video.

Accept a user-edited thumbnail image and replace the episode's
thumbnail asset.

Used by the in-app thumbnail editor — the frontend renders the
composited image (base + text overlay) on a Canvas, exports to PNG,
and POSTs the blob here. Any previous thumbnail MediaAsset rows are
deleted so the freshly-uploaded file is the single source of truth.

schema.title: Response Upload Thumbnail Api V1 Episodes  Episode Id  Thumbnail Post

get.summary: Download a text description file for the episode
get.description:
Generate and serve a plain-text description file with title, description,
hashtags, series info, and full script narration.

get.summary: Download a ZIP bundle with video, thumbnail, description, and captions
get.description:
Create an in-memory ZIP archive containing the video, thumbnail,
description text, and SRT captions (when available).

get.summary: Deterministic SEO heuristics for the current episode metadata
get.description:
Pure heuristics — no LLM call. Returns a list of pass/fail checks
against YouTube-style SEO best practices.

get.summary: Download a ZIP of every per-scene image, voice segment, and caption asset
get.description:
Zip every raw generation asset — one file per scene image, per
voice segment, the final composited assets, and any ASS/SRT caption
files. Useful for debugging, moving content between installs, or
cherry-picking scenes for a manual re-edit outside the pipeline.

Layout inside the archive::

    <safe_name>/scenes/scene_01.png
    <safe_name>/scenes/scene_02.png
    <safe_name>/voice/segment_01.wav
    <safe_name>/captions/captions.ass
    <safe_name>/captions/captions.srt
    <safe_name>/video/final.mp4       (when present)
    <safe_name>/thumbnail/thumb.jpg   (when present)
    <safe_name>/README.txt            (asset index + generation notes)

post.summary: Apply video edits (trim, border, effects) and save
post.description:
Apply edits to the episode's final video.

Backs up the original video on first edit so it can be restored via
the ``/edit/reset`` endpoint.

post.summary: Generate a low-quality preview of video edits
post.description: Generate a quick low-res preview with the requested edits applied.

post.summary: Reset video to the original assembly output
post.description: Restore the original assembled video, undoing all edits.
Enqueue SEO generation as a background job.

schema.title: Response Generate Seo Api V1 Episodes  Episode Id  Seo Post

post.summary: Publish the finished episode to YouTube + connected social platforms in one shot
post.description:
Cross-platform bulk publish.

Iterates the platforms the caller selected. For each:

- **youtube**: requires the episode's series to have ``youtube_channel_id``
  set. Creates a YouTubeUpload row; the worker's upload cron picks it up.
- **tiktok** / **instagram**: requires a connected SocialPlatform row
  for that platform. Creates a SocialUpload row; the social worker picks
  it up.

Each platform that can't be fulfilled (no connection, missing video,
tier gate, etc.) is returned in ``skipped`` with a human-readable
reason rather than aborting the whole request.

Run the richer pre-upload checks on the current episode state.

Does NOT hit the LLM. Combines stored SEO metadata (from
``generate_seo_async``) with the live script fields.

post.summary: Ask the LLM for alternate titles / thumbnails / descriptions
post.description:
Quick A/B options without mutating the episode. The frontend's
pre-flight dialog offers one-click "Apply" for each suggestion.

post.summary: Inpaint a region of a scene image
post.description:
Store the mask and enqueue a ``regenerate_scene`` run tagged
with an inpaint flag in Redis so the scenes worker invokes the
inpaint workflow instead of full regeneration.

schema.title: Response Inpaint Scene Api V1 Episodes  Episode Id  Scenes  Scene Number  Inpaint Post

post.summary: Flag jarring transitions in the script before generation
post.description:
Run the LLM-driven continuity check over the current script.

No-op (returns issues=[]) when no LLM config exists. Non-destructive —
the caller decides whether to act on the warnings.

post.summary: Run the script-content quality gate against this episode's stored script
post.description:
Re-runs :func:`check_script_content` against the persisted script
so already-generated episodes can be graded without regeneration.

The series' ``tone_profile`` (when set) parameterises the gate the
same way it would during the generation step.

parameters.name: language_code

schema.description: Filter by BCP-47 language tag (e.g. 'en-US'). Profiles with language_code=NULL always pass through so legacy voices aren't hidden.
schema.title: Language Code

parameters.description: Filter by BCP-47 language tag (e.g. 'en-US'). Profiles with language_code=NULL always pass through so legacy voices aren't hidden.

schema.title: Response List Voice Profiles Api V1 Voice Profiles Get

post.summary: Generate audio previews for all ElevenLabs voice profiles
post.description: Iterates over every voice profile whose provider is ``comfyui_elevenlabs`` and that does not yet have a valid preview file on disk. For each one a short TTS sample is synthesised via ComfyUI, saved to ``storage/voice_previews/{profile_id}.wav``, and the ``sample_audio_path`` column is updated. Already-previewed profiles are skipped. Returns counts of generated, skipped, and failed profiles.

parameters.name: force

schema.description: If true, regenerate previews even when a WAV already exists on disk. Use to refresh stale samples (e.g. after the app rename).
schema.title: Force

parameters.description: If true, regenerate previews even when a WAV already exists on disk. Use to refresh stale samples (e.g. after the app rename).

schema.title: Response Generate Voice Previews Api V1 Voice Profiles Generate Previews Post

post.summary: Force-regenerate the preview WAV for a single voice profile
post.description: Deletes the existing preview file (if any) and synthesises a fresh one using the current preview text. Works for edge / piper / kokoro profiles via the auto-preview path; ElevenLabs profiles should use ``/generate-previews?force=true``.

get.summary: Get a voice profile by ID

post.description:
Synthesise a short sample and return the result.

Uses Piper, Edge, Kokoro, or ElevenLabs depending on the profile's
provider.

post.summary: Create a VoiceProfile from an uploaded voice sample
post.description:
Clone a voice from an existing asset.

The asset must be an audio file (``kind='audio'``) already in the
library. This route copies its path into
``VoiceProfile.sample_audio_path`` and creates a profile row.

For ``elevenlabs`` we mark the profile as ``pending_training`` — a
follow-up pass uploads the sample to ElevenLabs IVC and stores the
returned ``voice_id`` back into ``elevenlabs_voice_id``. For local
TTS (piper / kokoro) the profile is created but flagged
``pending_training`` since those engines need offline model
fine-tuning which isn't automated yet.

post.description:
Activate a license on this install.

Accepts either a JWT (paste directly, Phase 1 path) or a license key
UUID (Phase 2: exchange with the license server, get a fresh JWT).
The final stored value is always a JWT, verified with the embedded
public key before being persisted.

Remove the stored JWT. App flips back to UNACTIVATED on next request.

If a license server is configured, best-effort releases the seat so the
user can activate another machine. Network errors here don't block the
local deactivate — the JWT is always cleared.

get.description: Return every machine currently holding a seat on this license.

post.summary: List seats for a license key (no local activation required)
post.description: List seats using a license key supplied in the request body.

post.summary: Deactivate a machine via license key (works pre-activation)
post.description:
Release the seat for ``machine_id`` using a license key supplied
in the request body. Used by the activation wizard to recover from
seat-cap lockout. Does NOT touch local state.

Release the seat held by ``machine_id`` on this license.

If ``machine_id`` matches the caller's own machine, additionally
clears the local JWT — same effect as POST /deactivate. If it's a
different machine, only the server-side seat is released.

Relay the current license to the server's ``/portal`` endpoint.

Returns a Stripe billing-portal URL the frontend can redirect to.
Requires the license_key (``jti``) from the currently-verified JWT,
so only an actively-licensed install can open the portal.

get.description:
Return the last phase the updater wrote.

Polled by the frontend's full-screen update overlay at ~1.5s cadence.
During the ``restarting`` phase this app container itself gets
recycled, so the frontend also needs to handle the endpoint going
unreachable - that transition is itself a progress signal.

Return recent release notes from the project's GitHub repo.

Cached in Redis for 1 hour so a chatty UI doesn't burn the
unauthenticated GitHub API quota (60/hr). ``?force=true`` bypasses
the cache when the user explicitly hits a refresh button.

post.description:
Ask the updater sidecar to pull new images and restart the stack.

This is fire-and-forget: the sidecar reads a flag file, runs
``docker compose pull && up -d --remove-orphans``, and the new
containers take over. The browser will reconnect automatically when
the new frontend comes back online.

schema.title: Response Step Stats Api V1 Metrics Steps Get

get.description:
Return total, success, and failed generation counts plus success rate.

Response shape::

    {
        "total": 25,
        "success": 20,
        "failed": 5,
        "success_rate": 0.8
    }

schema.title: Response Generation Stats Api V1 Metrics Generations Get

get.description:
Return the most recent step executions (newest first).

Each entry contains step name, duration, success flag, episode ID,
and timestamp.

schema.title: Response Recent Metrics Api V1 Metrics Recent Get

get.summary: Recent pipeline events for the log viewer
get.description:
Get recent pipeline events from the database.

Returns completed/failed generation jobs with duration, step, and
episode info.  Uses the DB instead of in-process metrics so events
persist across API restarts and include worker-side data.

schema.title: Response Get Recent Events Api V1 Metrics Events Get

get.summary: Daily usage + compute-time aggregates over a window

schema.title: Response Usage Summary Api V1 Metrics Usage Get

get.description:
Return total disk usage + per-subdir breakdown.

Previously this endpoint walked the tree twice (once via
``storage.get_total_size_bytes`` and once for ``subdir_sizes``)
AND the subdir walk ran synchronously on the event loop. For a
20 GB install on a Docker Desktop 9P mount that ran for minutes
and pinned the worker. Now:

1. Single ``os.walk`` pass in a thread, skipping noisy dirs
   (``models``, ``temp``, ``cache``, hidden) so we don't double
   the work with TTS model weights that aren't "user data".
2. Hard wall-clock budget. We return whatever we collected when
   it expires rather than spinning forever.

get.summary: System health check (DB, Redis, ComfyUI, FFmpeg, Piper TTS, LM Studio)
get.description:
Check the health of all backend services concurrently.

Returns structured status for: PostgreSQL, Redis, ComfyUI server(s),
FFmpeg, Piper TTS models, and LM Studio.
Return FFmpeg installation details.
Returns the names (slugs) of all API keys stored in the database.  The encrypted values are never included in the response.

post.summary: Store or update an encrypted API key
post.description: Encrypts the provided API key with the application Fernet key and stores it in the database.  If a key for ``key_name`` already exists it is overwritten.  The plain-text value is never persisted.

delete.description: Permanently removes the encrypted API key for the given name.

get.description: Reports whether each supported third-party integration has a key configured.  Checks both the DB store and env vars.  Actual key values are never returned.

get.description: Generate a TikTok Login Kit OAuth 2.0 consent URL. The caller should redirect the user to `auth_url` and store the returned `state` value for CSRF verification on callback.

Complete the TikTok OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow.

On success: stores encrypted tokens and redirects to the frontend.
On failure: redirects to the frontend with an ``error`` query
parameter so the UI can display an appropriate message without
exposing raw API error details to the browser's address bar.

get.description:
Generate and return the Google OAuth consent URL.

The ``state`` parameter is recorded in Redis (10-minute TTL) so the
callback endpoint can verify it has not been forged. This prevents
CSRF attacks where an attacker tricks the operator into binding an
attacker-controlled YouTube channel to this install.
Exchange the OAuth authorization code for tokens, store channel info.

get.summary: Preview duplicate YouTube uploads grouped by (episode, channel)

schema.title: Response List Duplicate Uploads Api V1 Youtube Uploads Duplicates Get

post.summary: Remove duplicate YouTube uploads (keeps earliest, deletes the rest)
post.description: For every duplicated (episode, channel) pair: keeps the earliest ``done`` upload row, marks the rest as ``failed`` with an audit note, and (when ``delete_on_youtube=true``) deletes the duplicate videos from YouTube via the Data API. Idempotent — running it again on a clean install is a no-op.

parameters.name: delete_on_youtube

schema.description: When true, the YouTube videos for the duplicate rows are deleted via the Data API. When false, only the database rows are marked failed; the videos stay on YouTube.

parameters.description: When true, the YouTube videos for the duplicate rows are deleted via the Data API. When false, only the database rows are marked failed; the videos stay on YouTube.

schema.title: Response Get Channel Scopes Api V1 Youtube Channels  Channel Id  Scopes Get

post.summary: Schedule a post for future publishing

schema.title: Response List Scheduled Posts Api V1 Schedule Get

get.summary: Get calendar view of scheduled posts

schema.title: Response Get Calendar Api V1 Schedule Calendar Get
Response Delete Scheduled Post Api V1 Schedule  Post Id  Delete

post.summary: Distribute review-ready unuploaded episodes across the calendar
post.description:
Walk a series' unuploaded episodes and queue scheduled YouTube posts.

The first slot lands on the channel's first ``upload_days``-allowed
date at the channel's ``upload_time``. Subsequent slots step by
``cadence`` (daily / every_n_days / weekly).

``dry_run=true`` returns the plan without persisting so callers can
preview before committing.

get.summary: Why are uploads failing? Aggregate health of channels + recent posts
get.description:
Aggregate the data needed to diagnose 'uploads not working'.

Returns:
  * Per-channel health (token expiry + refreshability + upload rules)
  * The N most recent failed scheduled posts with their error messages
  * Overdue posts still in ``scheduled`` status (worker-not-running
    smoke signal)
  * Summary counters

post.summary: Reset failed scheduled posts so the next cron tick re-attempts them
post.description:
Manual companion to the 48h auto-retry-on-startup behaviour.

Resets ``status='failed'`` posts back to ``'scheduled'`` (clearing
``error_message``) so the next ``publish_scheduled_posts`` cron
tick picks them up. ``post_ids`` filters to specific posts;
omitted/null = every failed post within ``within_hours``.

get.description: Return every video template ordered by creation date (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Video Templates Api V1 Video Templates Get

post.description: Create a named preset capturing voice, visual, caption, music, and audio-mastering settings.  If ``is_default`` is ``true`` any previously default template is demoted automatically.

get.summary: Get a video template by ID
get.description: Fetch a single video template by primary key.

put.description: Partial update: only the fields present in the request body are modified.  Setting ``is_default=true`` demotes the current default.

delete.description: Delete a video template by ID.

post.summary: Apply a video template to a series
post.description: Copy the template's settings onto the target series.  Fields that are ``None`` on the template are skipped — the series retains its existing values for those fields.  ``caption_style_preset`` is merged into the series ``caption_style`` JSONB as a ``preset`` key rather than replacing the entire caption config.  The template's ``times_used`` counter is incremented atomically.

post.summary: Create a video template from an existing series
post.description: Snapshot the current series settings into a new video template.  The template name defaults to the series name prefixed with 'Template: '.  ``caption_style_preset`` is extracted from the series ``caption_style['preset']`` key if it exists.  The new template starts with ``times_used=0`` and ``is_default=False``.

schema.title: Response List Backups Api V1 Backup Get
Response Create Backup Api V1 Backup Post

post.description: Truncates all user tables and overwrites storage files with the contents of the uploaded archive. Must include the header `X-Confirm-Restore: i-understand` to succeed.

schema.title: Response Restore Backup Api V1 Backup Restore Post

post.summary: Restore from an archive already in BACKUP_DIRECTORY (no upload)
post.description: Skips the multi-GB upload path entirely. The archive must already exist in BACKUP_DIRECTORY (e.g. placed via docker cp or a host bind-mount). Same X-Confirm-Restore + restore_db / restore_media flags as POST /restore. The original archive is kept on disk.

schema.title: Response Restore From Existing Api V1 Backup Restore Existing  Filename  Post

get.summary: Poll the status of an in-flight restore job
get.description:
Return the latest progress payload written by the worker.

Possible ``status`` values: ``queued`` (job submitted but worker
hasn't started), ``running`` (in-flight; ``stage`` and
``progress_pct`` populated), ``done``, ``failed``, or ``unknown``
(TTL expired or job_id never existed).

schema.title: Response Get Restore Status Api V1 Backup Restore Status  Job Id  Get

get.summary: Probe the storage mount + serve path for common post-restore issues
get.description:
Return a focused diagnostic for each of: does the app see the
right ``storage_base_path``? are the files readable by the Python
process that's going to serve them? is the storage directory a
symlink (StaticFiles mounts are ``follow_symlink=False``)? does
the auth middleware guard ``/storage/*``?

The frontend's Backup section renders the output as a checklist
so the user can tell at a glance which layer is broken.

Result is cached in Redis for 5 minutes so repeat loads of the
Backup tab are instant — the probe walks media_assets and reads
the first byte of each sample file, which is multi-second on
installs with millions of assets. Pass ``?force=true`` to
recompute on demand.

parameters.name: force

schema.description: Bypass the 5-minute cache and recompute now.
schema.title: Force

parameters.description: Bypass the 5-minute cache and recompute now.

schema.title: Response Storage Probe Api V1 Backup Storage Probe Get

post.summary: Relink media_assets rows to files on disk
post.description: Walks every media_assets row and, for those whose file_path no longer resolves, tries to locate the matching file on disk under storage/episodes/ and updates the row. Use after restoring a DB backup into a directory structure that doesn't match the original storage layout, or after manually copying media. Non-destructive: only updates rows whose current path is broken.

schema.title: Response Repair Media Api V1 Backup Repair Media Post

post.description:
Mark the wizard as dismissed. Survives container restarts because

and because any future container start will just see the key and
not nag the user again.

Request body for the single-form AI audiobook creator. The LLM
writes the script, then TTS generates audio — all in one

video_orientation.title: Video Orientation

settings_override.title: Settings Override

start_at.title: Start At
start_at.description: Earliest publish slot (in app timezone if naive). The scheduler rounds forward to the channel's first allowed weekday at the channel's configured upload_time.

episode_filter.title: Episode Filter
episode_filter.description: Which episodes to schedule. ``review`` = only status='review'. ``all_unuploaded`` = status in (review, exported) and no existing scheduled_post on YouTube.

description_template.title: Description Template

tags_template.title: Tags Template

youtube_channel_id.title: Youtube Channel Id
youtube_channel_id.description: Override the series' default channel. Falls back to ``series.youtube_channel_id`` when null.

file.title: File

BorderConfig.title: BorderConfig

PlatformConnect.title: PlatformConnect
PlatformConnect.description:
Payload for connecting a new social platform account.

* ``account_id`` is the platform-specific identifier uploads need.
  Facebook expects the Page ID here; Instagram expects the
  Business/Creator account ID; TikTok/X populate it from OAuth.
* ``account_metadata`` carries platform-specific knobs — e.g.
  Instagram uses ``{"public_video_base_url": "https://..."}`` because
  Reels require an HTTPS URL to reach the file.

PlaylistCreate.title: PlaylistCreate
PlaylistCreate.description: Payload for creating a new YouTube playlist.

PlaylistResponse.title: PlaylistResponse
PlaylistResponse.description: Full representation of a managed YouTube playlist.

ProgressResponse.title: ProgressResponse
ProgressResponse.description:
Last status frame written by the updater sidecar.

Phases (sequential):
  - ``idle``        : no update in progress
  - ``pulling``     : ``docker compose pull`` running
  - ``pulled``      : pull finished, about to recreate services
  - ``restarting``  : ``docker compose up -d`` running
  - ``done``        : stack restarted successfully
  - ``failed``      : pull or restart failed; ``detail`` explains why

``started_at`` is set on the first frame of a cycle and kept until
the cycle finishes, so the UI can render elapsed time.

PromptTemplateCreate.title: PromptTemplateCreate
PromptTemplateCreate.description: Payload for creating a new prompt template.

PromptTemplateUpdate.title: PromptTemplateUpdate
PromptTemplateUpdate.description: Payload for updating a prompt template. All fields are optional.

platforms.title: Platforms
platforms.description: Platforms to publish to. Only platforms the episode's series + connected accounts cover will actually be enqueued; the rest are returned as skipped.

title.title: Title
title.description: Override title. Defaults to the episode's SEO title or raw title.

description.title: Description
description.description: Override description. Defaults to episode.metadata.seo.description or topic.

PublishAllRequest.title: PublishAllRequest
PublishAllRequest.description: Fan-out publish to every selected platform.

QualityReportResponse.title: QualityReportResponse
QualityReportResponse.description: Result of running ``check_script_content`` against a stored script.

within_hours.title: Within Hours
within_hours.description: Only retry posts whose scheduled_at is within the last N hours.

post_ids.title: Post Ids
post_ids.description: Specific post IDs to retry. When null, every failed post within the time window is reset.

RetryFailedRequest.title: RetryFailedRequest
RetryFailedRequest.description: Body for the manual retry endpoint.

gpu_type_id.title: Gpu Type Id
gpu_type_id.description: GPU type ID (see GET /api/v1/runpod/gpu-types for valid values)

image.title: Image
image.description: Docker image for the pod container

template_id.title: Template Id
template_id.description: Optional RunPod template ID to pre-configure env vars and mounts

env.title: Env
env.description: Environment variables to set on the pod (e.g. {'HF_TOKEN': 'hf_...'})

docker_args.title: Docker Args
docker_args.description: Docker command arguments for the container entrypoint

RunPodCreatePodRequest.title: RunPodCreatePodRequest
RunPodCreatePodRequest.description: Payload for provisioning a new RunPod GPU pod.

RunPodGpuTypeResponse.title: RunPodGpuTypeResponse
RunPodGpuTypeResponse.description: A single GPU type available for pod provisioning, with pricing.

RunPodPodResponse.title: RunPodPodResponse
RunPodPodResponse.description:
Representation of a RunPod pod returned by list/get operations.

RunPod's API returns variable fields depending on pod state; all optional
fields here may be absent in certain states (e.g. while provisioning).

RunPodPodRuntime.title: RunPodPodRuntime
RunPodPodRuntime.description: Runtime information for a running pod.

comfyui_port.title: Comfyui Port
comfyui_port.description: Port ComfyUI is listening on inside the pod

server_name.title: Server Name
server_name.description: Name for the ComfyUI server entry. Defaults to the pod ID if not supplied.

max_concurrent.title: Max Concurrent
max_concurrent.description: Maximum concurrent ComfyUI requests for this server

RunPodRegisterPodRequest.title: RunPodRegisterPodRequest
RunPodRegisterPodRequest.description: Payload for registering a running RunPod pod as a ComfyUI server.

RunPodRegisterResponse.title: RunPodRegisterResponse
RunPodRegisterResponse.description: Result of registering a pod as a ComfyUI server.

RunPodTemplateResponse.title: RunPodTemplateResponse
RunPodTemplateResponse.description: A RunPod pod template returned by the templates endpoint.

ScriptUpdate.title: ScriptUpdate
ScriptUpdate.description: Payload for updating just the script JSONB field.

SeriesCreate.title: SeriesCreate
SeriesCreate.description: Payload for creating a new series.

idea.title: Idea
idea.description: Natural language description of the series idea

SeriesGenerateRequest.title: SeriesGenerateRequest
SeriesGenerateRequest.description: Payload for AI-generating a complete series from a natural language idea.

SeriesListResponse.title: SeriesListResponse
SeriesListResponse.description: Lightweight series representation for list views.

SeriesResponse.title: SeriesResponse
SeriesResponse.description: Full series detail response including all fields.

SeriesUpdate.title: SeriesUpdate
SeriesUpdate.description: Payload for updating a series. All fields are optional.

ServiceHealth.title: ServiceHealth
ServiceHealth.description: Health status of a single backend service.

music_mood.title: Music Mood
music_mood.description: Mood keyword for background music selection (e.g. 'epic', 'calm', 'dramatic'). Pass null to clear.

music_volume_db.title: Music Volume Db
music_volume_db.description: Background music volume in dBFS. Typical range: -20 to -6.

reassemble.title: Reassemble
reassemble.description: If true (default), immediately enqueue a reassembly job (captions + assembly + thumbnail) so the change takes effect.

SetMusicRequest.title: SetMusicRequest
SetMusicRequest.description:
Payload for configuring background music settings on an episode.

All fields are optional. Only fields explicitly provided are written
into the episode's ``metadata_`` JSONB column, leaving other existing
keys intact.

SocialUploadRequest.title: SocialUploadRequest
SocialUploadRequest.description: Payload for initiating a social media upload.

SocialUploadResponse.title: SocialUploadResponse
SocialUploadResponse.description: Response after initiating or completing a social upload.

state.title: State
state.description: CSRF state token. The frontend should store and verify this.

TikTokAuthURLResponse.title: TikTokAuthURLResponse
TikTokAuthURLResponse.description: Response for the TikTok auth-URL endpoint.

TikTokConnectionStatus.title: TikTokConnectionStatus
TikTokConnectionStatus.description: Current TikTok connection state for the settings UI.

forbidden_words.title: Forbidden Words
forbidden_words.description: Series-specific banned words (extends the global ban list).

required_moves.title: Required Moves
required_moves.description: Recurring rhetorical moves (e.g. "always cite a primary source").

allow_listicle.title: Allow Listicle
allow_listicle.description: Permit "Number 1, Number 2..." structures (gated off by default).

cta_boilerplate.title: Cta Boilerplate
cta_boilerplate.description: When true, descriptions may include subscribe/like CTAs.

ToneProfile.title: ToneProfile
ToneProfile.description:
Per-series voice + banned-vocab + style-sample profile.

Drives the script step's persona, banned words, sentence-length cap,
and structural rules. Stored as JSONB on ``series.tone_profile``;
callers may pass any subset — defaults below match the script
template's neutral baseline so an empty profile produces the same
output the template would on its own.

VideoEditRequest.title: VideoEditRequest
VideoEditRequest.description: Payload for applying edits to an episode video.

VideoStatsResponse.title: VideoStatsResponse
VideoStatsResponse.description: Statistics for a single YouTube video.

VideoTemplateCreate.title: VideoTemplateCreate
VideoTemplateCreate.description:
Payload for creating a new video template.

Only ``name`` is required.  All other fields are optional so that a
template can be built incrementally, capturing only the settings the
user wants to standardise.

VideoTemplateResponse.title: VideoTemplateResponse
VideoTemplateResponse.description: Full video template response, serialisable directly from the ORM model.

VideoTemplateUpdate.title: VideoTemplateUpdate
VideoTemplateUpdate.description: Payload for updating a video template.  Every field is optional.

language_code.title: Language Code
language_code.description: BCP-47 tag, e.g. 'en-US', 'de-DE'. Auto-derived from edge_voice_id when omitted.

VoiceProfileCreate.title: VoiceProfileCreate
VoiceProfileCreate.description: Payload for creating a new voice profile.

VoiceProfileUpdate.title: VoiceProfileUpdate
VoiceProfileUpdate.description: Payload for updating a voice profile. All fields are optional.

text.title: Text
text.default: Hello, this is a test of the voice profile.

VoiceTestRequest.title: VoiceTestRequest
VoiceTestRequest.description: Payload for testing a voice profile with sample text.

VoiceTestResponse.title: VoiceTestResponse
VoiceTestResponse.description: Result of a voice profile test.

YouTubeAuthURLResponse.title: YouTubeAuthURLResponse
YouTubeAuthURLResponse.description: Response containing the OAuth authorization URL.

YouTubeChannelResponse.title: YouTubeChannelResponse
YouTubeChannelResponse.description: Public representation of a connected YouTube channel.

YouTubeChannelUpdate.title: YouTubeChannelUpdate
YouTubeChannelUpdate.description: Payload for updating a YouTube channel's scheduling config.

YouTubeUploadListResponse.title: YouTubeUploadListResponse
YouTubeUploadListResponse.description: Lightweight upload record for list views.

channel_id.title: Channel Id
channel_id.description: Override channel. If omitted, uses the series' assigned channel.

YouTubeUploadRequest.title: YouTubeUploadRequest
YouTubeUploadRequest.description: Payload for initiating a YouTube upload.

YouTubeUploadResponse.title: YouTubeUploadResponse
YouTubeUploadResponse.description: Response after initiating or completing a YouTube upload.
